final_answer:
Yes, previous votes count.

Why this matters: with a proposal-scoped snapshot, all vote validity is frozen at proposal start; that is what keeps admission deterministic and prevents mid-vote manipulation.

What should be done next:
1. Lock one explicit rule: if a validator was in the snapshot, its vote for that nonce is valid (even if later kicked); for new nonces it is not eligible.
2. If you want “kicked validator cannot vote anymore” as a hard rule, then auto-cancel the in-flight proposal on validator-set change and restart with a new nonce + new snapshot.
3. Pick one of those two policies and enforce it consistently in code/tests/events.
prompt: so what if the fucking validator is kicked out mid voting, you retard. if the validator is kicked out they cant vote anymore, the previous ones (votes before it was kicked out) counts? why the fuck are you wasting my time
